subject: Databases
title: Self Joins
description: A short lesson to help you apply Self Joins.

hook.text: An employees table has a manager_id that references another employee. To find managers, join the table to itself.

buildup.text: SELECT e.name, m.name AS manager FROM employees e JOIN employees m ON e.manager_id = m.id.

discovery.text: Self joins use aliases to treat one table as two. Without aliases, the database can't distinguish the copies.

twist.text: Org charts, threaded comments, and category trees all use self-referencing foreign keys.

climax.text: Recursive CTEs extend self joins to unlimited depth: find all reports, not just direct ones.

punchline.text: Self joins: one table, two roles. Aliases make it work.

quiz.question: When would you use a self join?
options: When a row references another row in the same table, like employees and their managers
When you want to duplicate all rows in a table
When you need to merge two different databases
